config.section.go2rtc.label: go2rtc Cameras
config.section.go2rtc.desc: Secure managed camera streams, snapshots, and live viewing
config.go2rtc.proxy_note: AuraGo keeps camera URLs and go2rtc credentials private. Viewing uses the AuraGo proxy; direct LAN WebRTC is optional.
config.go2rtc.store_media: Store snapshots in the media registry
config.go2rtc.web_ui_enabled: Enable read-only go2rtc Web UI for admins
config.go2rtc.save_first: Save your changes before testing or viewing.
help.go2rtc.enabled: Manage the pinned go2rtc Docker sidecar and expose only AuraGo-controlled API routes.
help.go2rtc.auto_start: Create or start the managed container when AuraGo starts.
help.go2rtc.agent_access: Advertise the read-only go2rtc tool only while the authenticated API is reachable.
help.go2rtc.store_media: Store dated, hashed JPEG snapshots under AuraGo's data directory and register them as media.
help.go2rtc.web_ui_enabled: Allow administrators to open a read-only subset of the original go2rtc UI. Configuration and mutation routes remain blocked.
help.go2rtc.streams: Each stable stream has exactly one vault-only network source. Save changes before connection tests or previews.
help.go2rtc.stream_enabled: Disabled streams are removed from runtime reconciliation and cannot be viewed.
help.go2rtc.stream_id: Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, and underscores only. The ID is locked after a source is stored.
help.go2rtc.stream_name: Friendly name shown to users and the agent.
help.go2rtc.source: Allowed schemes: rtsp, rtsps, rtspx, http, https, and onvif. The complete URL is stored only in the encrypted Vault.
help.go2rtc.image: Reviewed default: go2rtc 1.9.14 with an immutable digest. Change only after auditing another release.
help.go2rtc.container_name: Name of the Docker container managed exclusively by AuraGo.
help.go2rtc.url: Internal sidecar URL. Leave empty for the Docker or loopback default.
help.go2rtc.api_host_port: For native AuraGo only; port 1984 is always bound to 127.0.0.1.
help.go2rtc.webrtc_enabled: Publish port 8555 over TCP and UDP for direct browser media. HTTP proxy viewing remains available.
help.go2rtc.webrtc_bind: Required concrete private LAN IP used both for Docker publishing and as the WebRTC candidate.
help.go2rtc.webrtc_port: Host port published for WebRTC TCP and UDP. Default: 8555.
